Der Blöde Dritte Mittwoch #92 takes place on December 18, 2019, at grillX in Vienna. The event poster displays the lineup of artists: <image 1>
The evening features three acts. Sophia Goidinger-Koch, born in 1987 in Innsbruck and based in Vienna, is a freelance artist in contemporary and improvised music. She has collaborated with renowned figures like Vinko Globokar and performed at major festivals. She presents a 'solo violin' performance.
Danielle Pamp performs 'Elegi', a piece focusing on gender and identity, questioning archetypes of the masculine and feminine through operatic voice.
Paul Gründorfer and Clemens Hausch present 'AS–MR', exploring sound as a tactile phenomenon using custom electronic devices.
Pictured below is a portrait of the violinist Sophia Goidinger-Koch.

My boys are fascinated with robots, so we felt inspired to build our own using recycled materials. Here is the finished robot. <image 1>
We drew inspiration from various robot books and a model in a non-fiction book. We constructed the body from a granola bar box and a cookie box. The robot is double-sided, with my oldest son's artwork on one side and my youngest's on the other. We covered the head box in foil, wrapping it like a present, and used plastic Easter egg halves with drawn-on pupils for the eyes. <image 2>
For the neck, we connected the head to the body using a toilet paper roll covered in scrapbook paper. The arms were made from foil-covered toilet paper rolls wrapped in bubble wrap and taped to the body. <image 3>
Finally, we created the legs using plastic fruit cups. We were too impatient to paint them, so they remain clear. We glued these fruit cup legs to a piece of cardboard covered in foil to provide a stable base for the robot.

The SaniDry™ Basement Air System serves as one of the most effective dehumidifiers available, designed to control moisture and lower humidity to prevent mold and dust mites. This high-capacity system offers consistent, high-efficiency performance. <image 1> The unit combines dehumidification with air filtration, removing up to 100 pints of water a day while using the energy of a standard 40-pint dehumidifier. It filters airborne particles down to two microns, trapping mold spores and dust. The powerful blower moves dry air throughout the basement to eliminate dampness. The system is equipped with a large air filter, shown here being accessed for maintenance.

This Fireproof Large Gun Safe Cabinet provides secure storage for up to 5 rifles and 2 pistols. It features a deeper design to accommodate scoped rifles up to 50 inches and includes a separate internal lock box for smaller valuables. <image 1> The security system utilizes a reliable electronic keypad, allowing for custom PIN codes, and includes a backup key for emergency access. <image 2> Built with high-strength, integrally welded steel, the safe features a tamper-proof inner edge and 3 solid live-action locking bolts to prevent prying and theft. <image 3> Inside, the layout includes a removable shelf and a sponge gun holder to organize and protect firearms effectively. <image 4> Designed for ease of use and maximum security, the safe can be mounted to the floor or wall. The final view displays the safe in both its open and locked configurations, highlighting the sleek exterior and spacious interior.

As we head into the coldest, driest part of the year, I adjust my skincare routine to maintain moisture for my dry skin. Here is the collection of products I rely on. <image 1>
Step 1: To start, I tie my hair back with a headband and wear a robe to keep my clothes dry. <image 2>
Step 2: I remove any makeup using 100% Extra Virgin Coconut Oil. The oil breaks down makeup effectively while adding moisture, preventing the need for harsh tugging on the skin. <image 3>
Step 3: I wipe away the oil and makeup with a gentle cleansing wipe.
Step 4: I cleanse my face with Neutrogena Hydro Boost Cleanser. I love this product because it cleanses without stripping natural oils, and a little goes a long way. <image 4>
Step 5: I apply a hydrating toner to reset the skin.
Step 6: I massage in a face oil or serum. Occasionally, I use a jade roller to help the oil penetrate deeply and to depuff my face.
Step 7: I lock in the hydration with a daily facial moisturizer.
Although I've finished the routine, here is a look back at the cleansing step, showing the rich lather of the Hydro Boost Cleanser on my face.

WASHINGTON — It was 1962 when D.C. pulled the plug on the city's trolley system and shuttered the Dupont Circle station. Other than a short stint as a fallout shelter in the 1960s and a food court in the 1990s, the station remained largely unused for more than 50 years. <image 1> <image 2> For months now, the Arts Coalition for the Dupont Underground has been in the forgotten tunnels with tracks, with the goal of adding art and welcoming the public in this year. <image 3> "It's a way to show the world that this is a top-tier art city," says Philippa Hughes, with the Arts Coalition for Dupont Underground. The group is leasing the station from the city and plans to open its doors on April 30. The first exhibition is called "Raise/Raze," which will consist of 650,000 plastic balls used during the Beach Exhibit last summer at the National Building Museum. <image 4> <image 5> "People will be able to come down and see cubes everywhere and they can build them into different structures," Hughes says. <image 6> Hou de Sousa, a firm from New York, won a design competition for the exhibit, and they will begin putting it together this weekend. Hughes said volunteers have already begun work making the cubes which will be used to assemble the exhibit. Getting the tunnel to this point has involved countless volunteer hours and has uncovered some interesting items, including tin cans with crackers from its time as a fallout shelter. <image 7> "There was a dentist chair … who knows why that was here," Hughes says. The $400,000 project has been supported mostly through crowd funding and is part of a five-year lease from the city to Arts Coalition for the Dupont Underground. Tickets for the first event, which runs from April 30 through June 1, are $15 and can be gotten here.

Dior has released the Diorskin Star Foundation, featuring intelligent light-reflecting pigments that brighten the skin and correct dark spots while offering SPF 30 protection. The product line is presented in chic, square glass packaging that reflects the brand's elegant aesthetic. <image 1> <image 2> <image 3> The foundation bottle is designed with a pump for easy dispensing and contains a lightweight, liquid formula that builds from medium to full coverage. <image 4> It sets to a luminous matte finish that looks natural and seamless. The swatches here demonstrate the texture and coverage of the shades. <image 5> Complementing the foundation is the Diorskin Star Foundation Sponge and the Diorskin Star Concealer. The concealer is designed to neutralize dark circles and illuminate the eye contour with a hydrating, stretch-effect formula, packaged in a sleek tube. <image 6> To ensure precise application, the concealer comes with a built-in sponge tip applicator, as shown in the open tube, which allows for effortless blending and full coverage.

Shenzhen Dragon Glass, founded in 1994, is a professional manufacturer dedicated to providing excellent quality tempered glass to clients worldwide. We focus on every process to ensure perfection, with a large output that keeps our prices competitive. <image 1> Our installation process ensures safety. The tempering process involves heating edge-worked glass to over 600 degrees and cooling it with strong wind jets, creating high surface pressure and balanced interior tension to increase strength fivefold. <image 2> The resulting glass is super flat, distortion-free, and has high transmittance. It is tough enough to endure powerful impacts. If it does break, it shatters into small, blunt fragments rather than sharp shards, ensuring no harm to humans. <image 3> Here is a view of our production facilities: <image 4> <image 5> We conduct careful quality control inspections, including checks on size, surface, and stress, to ensure perfection before delivery. <image 6> We adopt top-grade raw materials and utilize precise processing techniques, such as drilling holes with accurate sizes and positioning. <image 7> Our tempered glass is available in various colors and thicknesses, suitable for facades, railings, and windows. After passing impact testing and stress inspections, the products are ready for shipment. We use strong plywood crates to ensure the safety of our glass products during long-distance transportation.

Join 'The Greatest' Ocean Road tour to experience a story of ancient culture, shipwrecks, and geology. This loop covers the entire trail, including the Grampians and the Bellarine Peninsula. <image 1>

We explore the massive limestone cliffs and descend the Gibson Steps to walk on the pristine sands below. <image 2>

Our journey is a complete loop starting and ending in Melbourne, hitting every major highlight along the coast and inland. <image 3>

I know this area intimately, from the popular surf beaches to the quiet, sun-drenched shores. <image 4>

We trek through the ancient rainforests of the Otways, surrounded by lush ferns and towering trees. <image 5>

Witness the iconic Twelve Apostles rising majestically from the Southern Ocean. <image 6>

Enjoy moments of solitude gazing out at the endless horizon <image 7>

and explore hidden rock arches and caves along the rugged coastline. <image 8>

The tour includes all meals, transport, and camping gear—just bring your hiking shoes, warm clothes, and a sense of adventure. We finish with one last look at the breathtaking coast that makes this tour unforgettable.

Produced by John Carrow around 1884, this silverplate tea service features foliate motifs including roses, urn finials, and elaborate scrolled feet. <image 1> <image 2> The set includes a large cream pitcher <image 3>, two identical pots <image 4>, and a waste bowl <image 5>. The pieces show original thin lacquer with light flaking, but the plating is well-preserved. Detailed views highlight the floral bands <image 6>, scrolled feet <image 7>, and handle attachments <image 8>. The items are clearly marked and numbered '2760', 'Triple Plate', with a patent date of February 1, 1876. <image 9>

This Eighties desk lamp by Stephan Copeland for Arteluce Italy is shown here illuminated <image 1>. It features black plastic and anthracite metal construction <image 2>. The flexible arms allow it to extend horizontally <image 3>. The lamp uses a halogen bulb <image 4>, and the cast iron base is clearly labeled <image 5>. It can be positioned upright to a height of 110cm <image 6>, utilizing durable flexible joints <image 7>. The head has a ribbed texture <image 8> and a sculpted form <image 9>. Ideally suited for a desk, the lamp is in excellent condition .
